channel.title: WA Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: 'Terrible tragedy': Minister won't request coronial inquest into baby's death
item.description: WA's health minister says she would support a coronial inquest into the death of an eight-month-old girl at Kalgoorlie Health Campus, but says it is a decision for the state coroner.

media:group.media:description: Baby Willow Katarina Horne died after being admitted to the emergency department at Kalgoorlie Health Campus.

item.title: Bronze tugboat propellers worth $2m stolen in WA's north
item.description: In a heist not seen before in this WA mining town, thieves have allegedly made away with eight tonnes of tugboat propellers, with the copper estimated to be worth an eye-watering $2 million.

media:group.media:description: Police say the allegedly stolen tugboat propellers will cost $2 million to replace.

item.title: Woman found safe after five-day search in country WA
item.description: The desperate search for a 46-year-old woman who disappeared in southern Western Australia last week ends with her being found safe and well.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Chounding, Peter Barr, and Paul Cook

media:group.media:description: Jodie King has been found safe and well after being missing since Wednesday.

item.title: Hastie talks tough on One Nation while Taylor hesitates
item.description: As Angus Taylor grapples with the Coalition's dilemma of how to keep the centre while diverting voters away from Pauline Hanson's party, Andrew Hastie lays out his vision for fighting One Nation.

media:group.media:description: Andrew Hastie laid out his vision for fighting One Nation in the clearest terms.

item.title: Court shutdown to end after Department of Justice resolves security dispute
item.description: Regional West Australian courts closed amid a stoush over security services are set to reopen about a month earlier than originally planned.

media:group.media:description: The courthouse in Warburton in the Goldfields has been among those closed since the start of June.

item.title: Woman attacked by pack of dogs says owners did nothing to help
item.description: A woman who says she was attacked by a pack of dogs that were being walked off-leash in Western Australia's far north is calling for more responsible pet ownership.
item.dc:creator: Giulia Bertoglio

media:group.media:description: The woman says she was attacked by seven dogs on a dirt track off Weaber Plain Road in Kununurra.

item.title: Report finds 'extensive' coal and gas presence in schools and children's sport
item.description: Santos, BHP, Woodside and others have an "extensive presence" in childhood settings through more than 260 programs, a new report identifies, as senators say classroom programs and lessons written by the industry should be examined through a senate inquiry.
item.dc:creator: Jake Evans

media:group.media:description: A report has identified more than 260 mining-backed programs in children's settings, saying taken together it shows a network of influence on young people.

item.title: Jackson the star as Dockers make it 14 wins in a row
item.description: The Fremantle Dockers keep rolling along, winning their 14th straight game with a dominant 51-point victory over Gold Coast at Perth Stadium â€” a result that puts them two games clear at the top of the AFL ladder.

media:group.media:description: Luke Jackson of the Dockers celebrates a goal during the round 16 AFL match between Fremantle Dockers and Gold Coast Suns at Optus Stadium, on June 28, 2026, in Perth, Australia. (Photo by Janelle St Pierre/AFL Photos/via Getty Images)

item.title: Dozens of former Australian Rules footballers diagnosed with CTE
item.description: Australian Rules footballers who never played in the game's premier league are being diagnosed with CTE, revealing that the brain trauma crisis striking all levels of the game.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Halloran, Amy Donaldson, and Dylan Welch

media:group.media:description: The AFL says it will explore all avenues to lower players' exposure to head trauma during games and training sessions.

item.title: Thai teenager's father 'deeply saddened' as Australian charged with murder
item.description: The family of a 17-year-old girl in Thailand speak of their grief as an Australian man is charged with her murder.
item.dc:creator: Elissa Fitzgerald

media:group.media:description: Thongchai Donholma and Oradee Bussarakum, the father and stepmother of Tunchanok Donhomla, spoke of their grief outside a police station in Pattaya.

item.title: Disruption-plagued Perth-to-Bunbury train service resumes after years of delays
item.description: The disruption-plagued Perth-to-Bunbury train service is back on track following years of breakdowns and delays, but locals remain frustrated over the rollout.

media:group.media:description: The new Australind train services will be doubled from 2027.

item.title: Woman feared 'being sent to hell' if she escaped cult, court told
item.description: An alleged victim of cult leader William "Little Pebble" Kamm has told a court she began experiencing extreme anxiety as she pondered an escape from a property on the NSW South Coast.
item.dc:creator: Grace Crivellaro

media:group.media:description: William Kamm (centre) outside the Downing Centre Court in Sydney.

item.title: Bruce Lehrmann loses legal bid for Brittany Higgins documentary footage
item.description: Former Liberal staffer Bruce Lehrmann has been knocked back in a bid to gain access to raw documentary footage which featured Brittany Higgins.
item.dc:creator: Talissa Siganto

media:group.media:description: Bruce Lehrmann had sought to get hold of raw documentary footage from a documentary featuring Brittany Higgins.

item.title: Expanded World Cup called 'vulgar and ordinary' by Ghana coach. Is that fair?
item.description: Ghana's coach Carlos Queiroz has lashed out at FIFA's expanded 48-team World Cup, despite his team progressing. Is his criticism justified as the tournament moves into the knockout stage?

media:group.media:description: Cabo Verde players celebrate progressing to the knockout stage.

item.title: Les Mills, NZ Olympian and founder of global fitness brand, dies aged 91
item.description: The New Zealand Olympian who opened an Auckland gym in 1968 that grew into an international group fitness brand has died aged 91.

media:group.media:description: Les Mills was a four-time Olympic athlete for New Zealand before launching his international fitness brand.

item.title: Gun laws, dating gods and polar bears: This week's Race Around The World
item.description: From survival vaults in Norway to a pianist in Pisa, catch up on the films from week four of Race Around The World.

media:group.media:description: A still from Jayden's film.

item.title: Key observatory shut for months prior to bird flu detection
item.description: Volunteers and wildlife advocates push to reopen the Eyre Bird Observatory, arguing the station will be critical in monitoring the potential impact of avian flu.

media:group.media:description: The remote volunteer-run station is the only one of its kind along WA's south coast.

item.title: NASA to bump telescope back into space to avoid it falling to Earth
item.description: US space agency NASA is racing to save an aging telescope, used to observe cosmic explosions, from falling back to Earth with a daring rescue mission.

media:group.media:description: After more than 20 years in space, NASA's Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory is slowly falling back to Earth.

item.title: History quiz: Test your knowledge from this decade to the 1600s
item.description: This week was a big one in times gone by. From this decade to the 17th century, put your history knowledge to the test.

media:group.media:description: Eminem, who won best rap album, performs onstage at the 53rd annual Grammy Awards in Los Angeles, California, on February 13, 2011.

item.title: How Ben overcame his dilemma about disclosing his autism in job interviews
item.description: The cybersecurity sector is projected to need tens of thousands of roles filled by 2030. Now, a university-based program is helping provide those workers by tapping into the abilities of autistic people.

media:group.media:description: Ben says he has a sense of belonging he has not always felt.

item.title: Williams takes aim at anti-doping measures ahead of Wimbledon return
item.description: As she prepares for her All England Club comeback, Serena Williams claims the tennis anti-doping protocol is "unprofessional" and "unreasonable".

media:group.media:description: Serena Williams is unhappy with the anti-doping protocol used by the governing bodies of tennis.

item.title: Tech revival sees ASX close 0.7pc higher, Neuren leaps on EU approval hopes â€” as it happened
item.description: The ASX shrugged off a sluggish Wall Street lead to close 0.7 per cent higher as Neuren Pharmaceuticals surged on a potential EU approval for one of its drugs.

media:group.media:description: ASX Board. Bridge st Sydney

item.title: France records 1,000 excess daily deaths in record European heatwave
item.description: More than 150 million Europeans swelter in "extreme heat", which scientists say has been the worst the continent has ever recorded.

media:group.media:description: France has sweltered through its most intense heatwave ever.

item.title: Ben Stokes's retirement a perfect example of his main character energy
item.description: Ben Stokes has been the centre of England's cricketing universe for 15 years. In announcing his retirement mid-match, he highlighted how damaging that main character energy can be.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Ben Stokes has never been afraid of stepping into the limelight.

item.title: WA shearing teams look for new ways to make ends meet
item.description: Almost 100 shearing shed workers across regional WA sign up to a new training program aimed at helping contractors and their teams find work beyond the shearing industry.Â

media:group.media:description: The WA Shearing Industry Association's Beyond the Shed program has attracted more than ninety shearers looking to diversify their skills and career options.
